title: Toxic Caterpillars Just Shut Berlin's Parks
script: Berlin's parks are closed! Not for a protest or a concert — for caterpillars. Specifically: oak processionary moth caterpillars. They've invaded Berlin's green spaces, and they look harmless. They are not. Each one carries about 700,000 microscopic barbed hairs. Those hairs break off in the wind and land on human skin, causing rashes, burning eyes, and breathing difficulties. Children have had severe reactions. Elderly residents are afraid to leave their homes. Berlin's second-largest park, Jungfernheide, has 2,000 trees cordoned off. Playgrounds closed. Sports fields shut down. The cleanup crew isn't using spray. They're wearing full protective suits — the same type used for asbestos — and vacuuming nests off branches 20 meters in the air. Climate change is making this worse every year. How long before Berlin can't keep up?
